metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_2431
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Administration of extracellular L-DOPA in a L-DOPA challenge model causes dramatic increase in DOPAC levels in GFP alone but not GFP-Rab3B expressing striatum as measured by microdialysis. Furthermore, overexpression of Rab3B increases both the number and size of synaptic vesicles in the synaptic terminals of these neurons.

8/2/2018 Pim
- Based on the known lipid anchor of the protein, the "anchored component of ... membrane" term was chosen.. Experimental description: Materials and methods: "Female Sprague-Dawley rats weighing 200-250 g
(Charles River Laboratories) were used in all animal experiments."

8/2/2018 PIm
- Overexpressed RAB3B is of unknown species. Authors contacted for species information (dd 8/2/2018, ole_isacson@hms.harvard.edu). Answer:

"Dear Prof Nierop,
Thank you for spearheading the SynGo project. I did answer the same email not so long ago but I'm wondering my email somehow did not reach you for some reason.
The RAB3B gene we used for the study was human (Homo Sapiens) origin.
Let me know if you need any other information.
Thank you.
Chee Yeun"

checks.label: OWL Consistency
checks.error_message: No inferences attributed to this model graph in the materializer output. Likely a parse failure or other materializer skip — re-run on this model alone to investigate.
